title: septas simulans
intro: Septas simulans, commonly known as the "false gentian," is a plant species native to tropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and alleviating respiratory issues. The therapeutic actions of Septas simulans include its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to relieve coughs and congestion, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Septas simulans include iridoids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties and health benefits.
intro_study: According to "Zhongguo Zhong yao za zhi = Zhongguo zhongyao zazhi = China journal of Chinese materia medica", Septas simulans benefits, as observed in the study, include improving the local pulmonary tissue levels of alpha1-antitrypsin, which can help prevent the formation of emphysema.
